mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points within a three-month window signals an abrupt labor market cooling that triggers recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns exceeding 3% in the subsequent month.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of housing starts and payroll employment indicates synchronized demand destruction across durable goods and services sectors, which systematically reduces corporate revenue growth expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample backtests, or forward returns remain statistically indistinguishable from zero across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of supply chain normalization, a sustained increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding capacity utilization that directly boosts corporate margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The positive return correlation reverses during supply-constrained regimes, or the signal yields a negative median forward return in the specified regime over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When real policy rates turn negative (FEDFUNDS falls below CPIAUCSL growth) while consumer credit expands (RSAFS rises), liquidity injections successfully stimulate equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < CPIAUCSL and RSAFS month-over-month change > 0
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with credit expansion
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative or flat when the trigger activates, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over FEDFUNDS alone in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in retail and food services sales (RSAFS) reflects resilient consumer spending that supports near-term earnings revisions, mechanically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The signal produces a negative median forward return over any 12-month rolling window, or the rank correlation with SPX 5-day returns falls below 0.02 consistently.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5 percent, it signals a structural shift in labor market slack that forces the Federal Reserve to prioritize growth support over inflation control, ultimately compressing discount rates and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain negative or statistically insignificant after the threshold breach, or the signal fails to capture the equity rally typically associated with Fed pivot expectations.

mechanism_narrative: The divergence where real GDP growth accelerates while industrial production stagnates indicates a demand-driven expansion fueled by services and consumption rather than manufacturing capacity, which historically supports equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The spread between GDP growth and industrial production fails to predict equity returns, or forward returns turn negative when the divergence widens, indicating a supply-constrained growth scare.
